message: Rewrite README to lead with user value

files.patch:
@@ -1,92 +1,80 @@
 # 🚀 clattio
 
-A command-line interface for managing Attio from your terminal.
+A command-line interface for managing [Attio](https://attio.com) from your terminal.
 
-This exists to fill a gap in the current MCP workflow: the MCP can read Attio well, but it cannot perform object writes. This CLI fixes that by exposing Attio's API as shell commands you can use directly or automate in scripts.
+Create, update, and inspect Attio objects, records, lists, entries, notes, tasks, webhooks, and more — without hand-writing REST calls.
 
-Use it to:
+## 🏁 Get started
 
-- create, update, and inspect Attio objects, records, lists, entries, and integration resources such as files, webhooks, and SCIM users
-- perform Attio write operations that are blocked through the MCP path
-- script Attio workflows with raw JSON output
-- manage Attio from the shell without hand-writing REST calls
-
-## ⚙️ Setup
+Grab an API token from your [Attio workspace settings](https://app.attio.com), then:
 
 ```sh
-bun install
-bun run generate
-bun run build
-bun run test
+bunx clattio auth token set
+bunx clattio objects list
+bunx clattio records search --object people --query "Jane"
 ```
 
-## ▶️ Run
-
-Run the built CLI directly:
+For a persistent global command:
 
 ```sh
-bun run build
-node dist/attio.js --help
-node dist/attio.js objects list
+npm install -g clattio
+attio objects list
 ```
 
-Run it without a build during development:
+## 🔐 Authenticate
+
+**API token** (recommended for scripts and quick usage):
 
 ```sh
-bun src/bin/attio.ts --help
-bun src/bin/attio.ts objects list
+attio auth token set
 ```
 
-Install the published package globally:
+**OAuth** (for apps that act on behalf of a user):
 
 ```sh
-npm install -g clattio
-attio --help
+export ATTIO_CLIENT_ID=...
+attio auth login
 ```
 
-Install it as a global command from this checkout:
+When `ATTIO_CLIENT_SECRET` is not set, the CLI prompts for it securely.
+On macOS, tokens and secrets are stored in Keychain by default.
 
-```sh
-bun link
-attio --help
-```
+## 🧭 OAuth setup
 
-## 🔐 Authenticate
+`attio auth login` opens the browser, handles the localhost callback, exchanges the authorization code, and stores the resulting token. You need an Attio OAuth app first:
 
-Use an API token:
+1. Go to [build.attio.com](https://build.attio.com) and create an app.
+2. In the app settings, find the OAuth section.
+3. Copy the `client_id` and `client_secret`.
+4. Register `http://127.0.0.1:8787/callback` as a redirect URI.
 
-```sh
-attio auth token set
-attio objects list
-```
+## ▶️ Run locally
 
-Or use the built-in OAuth flow:
+From a source checkout:
 
 ```sh
-export ATTIO_CLIENT_ID=...
-attio auth login
+bun src/bin/attio.ts --help
+bun src/bin/attio.ts objects list
 ```
 
-When `ATTIO_CLIENT_SECRET` is not set, the CLI prompts for it securely instead of accepting it on the command line.
-On macOS, stored OAuth secrets and tokens use Keychain by default.
-
-## 🧭 Attio OAuth Setup
-
-`attio auth login` can open the browser, handle the localhost callback, exchange the authorization code, and store the resulting token. It still requires an Attio OAuth app first.
+Or build first and run with Node:
 
-Get the OAuth credentials from the Attio developer dashboard:
+```sh
+bun run build
+node dist/attio.js objects list
+```
 
-1. Go to `https://build.attio.com`
-2. Create an app
-3. Open the app settings
-4. Find the OAuth settings
-5. Copy the `client_id` and `client_secret`
-6. Register `http://127.0.0.1:8787/callback` as a redirect URI for the CLI callback
+## ⚙️ Develop
 
-## 🔗 Attio Docs
+```sh
+bun install
+bun run generate
+bun run build
+bun run test
+```
 
-Relevant Attio docs:
+## 🔗 Attio docs
 
-- `https://docs.attio.com/docs/oauth/authorize`
-- `https://docs.attio.com/docs/oauth/token`
-- `https://docs.attio.com/rest-api/tutorials/connect-an-app-through-oauth`
+- [OAuth authorization](https://docs.attio.com/docs/oauth/authorize)
+- [OAuth token exchange](https://docs.attio.com/docs/oauth/token)
+- [Connect an app through OAuth](https://docs.attio.com/rest-api/tutorials/connect-an-app-through-oauth)
